rationale.R1.2: The request to clean up the passage centrally includes resolving internal tensions such as the training-free framing versus the later trained scoring head.
rationale.R1.6: Clean this up most directly asks for sentence- and paragraph-level improvement in clarity, flow, concision, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any cleanup should preserve uncertainty around inferred intent and unsupported interpretation of the section's claims.
rationale.RX.2: The revision responsibility would include avoiding stronger deployment, novelty, or causality claims than the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is broad but still limited to cleaning the provided paper text rather than evaluating the whole project or executing experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Changes should avoid introducing misleading claims that could distort downstream research or deployment decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected cleanup responsibility should preserve traceability to the given artifact and not invent external evidence.
